extraction.one_line_summary: VINE Jay attacked from breakaway on Cat-1 finish climb, won by 43 seconds over SOLER, while EVENEPOEL defended red jersey by following ROGLIC/MAS attacks.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL rode tempo control rather than attacking on final climb, yet distanced ROGLIC and MAS without explosive efforts.
VINE overwhelmed SOLER in final 175m despite Soler being only 20 seconds back at 700m mark, revealing superior punch.
AYUSO struggled 20-40 seconds behind red jersey group on final climb, exposing weakness compared to stage 6 form.
ROGLIC moved into third overall but couldn't match EVENEPOEL's tempo riding, concerning GC signal for Quick-Step's rival.

rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: second stage win in three days confirms elite climbing status, not just breakaway opportunist
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: defended jersey by tempo control alone without attacking, dropped ROGLIC/MAS/YATES through sustained power
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: dropped 20-40 seconds behind red jersey group, clearly weaker than stage 6 mountain finish performance
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: bridged to breakaway and took second but faded badly in final 700m despite being close
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: moved to third GC but couldn't respond when EVENEPOEL simply rode tempo, troubling for overall ambitions
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Carlos: positive: moved into fourth overall, matching EVENEPOEL's pace better than more established climbers
